title: NOC 21101 – Chemists | GTR Immigration
description: Chemists are essential to research, pharmaceuticals, environmental protection, manufacturing, and advanced materials across Canada. Classified under NOC 21101 (TEER 1), this profession remains in steady national demand—supporting immigration pathways through Express Entry–linked streams, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and employer-driven work permits.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Conducting quality-control testing of raw and finished materials
Developing new chemical formulations and industrial processes
Studying drug mechanisms and health impacts
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in chemistry, biochemistry, or related field
Master’s or PhD for research roles
Provincial licensing required in Quebec; voluntary in some other provinces
job_outlook: Between 2022–2031, Canada is projected to create 10,100 new chemist positions, while around 9,600 workers will enter the labour market—indicating stable to favourable immigration prospects for internationally trained professionals.
additional_information: Chemists continue to be sought across both private and public sectors, particularly in:
Pharmaceutical and biotechnology firmsPetrochemical and chemical manufacturersEnvironmental testing labsUniversities and research institutesGovernment agenciesMining, pulp & paper, and utilities
Annual salaries typically range between $40,100–$123,200 CAD, depending on specialization, province, and seniority.
Why Choose Canada as a Chemist?

Strong R&D investment nationwidePR-linked skilled-worker immigration programsAdvanced laboratory infrastructureCompetitive compensationFree public healthcareFamily sponsorship pathwaysMulticultural working environments
